title: Developer shrinks Syracuse apartment project near I-81 for second time
summary: Developer Christopher Geiger has reduced the Gateway Apartments project near I-81 for the second time, cutting units from 291 to 259 (11% reduction) after learning the eastern portion of the site would not qualify for state brownfield cleanup tax credits. The seven-story project at East Fayette and Almond streets remains a priority for the Almond Street corridor post-I-81 demolition, but financing and investor commitments are still pending with no construction timeline set.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Transportation

political_signal.description: Project remains aligned with city's I-81 corridor redevelopment vision, but repeated scope reductions and deferred IDA application suggest financing challenges and investor hesitancy. State brownfield policy limitations are constraining project economics.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts extracted: $129.8M (project cost estimate), $24M (IDA tax break request), $21M implied difference from prior reporting
Key institutional dependency: New York State Brownfield Cleanup Program eligibility determination directly impacted project viability
Project timeline uncertainty: No groundbreaking expected in 2025; financing still pending
Historic preservation considerations: Landmark Preservation Board involvement noted; AME Zion Church and XO Taco building preservation status relevant
Missing data: Developer has not yet submitted revised cost estimates or updated IDA application
Tag warning: Unknown tag 'brownfield-remediation' not in taxonomy
